A storm damage assessment team from the National Weather Service in Los Angeles/ Oxnard, California confirmed that an EF-0 tornado occurred in southeast Oxnard on 2/13/25. The estimated peak wind was 85 mph, path length 0.63 miles, path width 20 yards, duration 2 minutes. Visible damage could be seen across around a dozen double-wide mobile homes. Damage included loss of shingles and roof coverings, many destroyed metal porch roofs and carports, toppled fences, and two shattered windows. Debris was widespread across a significant portion of the community. Several large branches broke off of nearby trees across Highway 1 adjacent to the mobile home parks. 0 injuries and 0 fatalities from the tornado.

Local residents and field reporters confirm water from San Ysidro Creek is spilling over the banks at lower Jameson Lane, inundating the street.
Ventura County Sheriff reports active mud and sediment flows across Adohr Lane. A hard closure is in place; the roadway is impassable.
Media and field reports confirm hillside soil has moved onto the roadway, causing a major traffic hazard and partial blockage.
CHP confirmed rocks and mud have fallen across the lanes between PCH and Mulholland, forcing a transition to one-way controlled traffic. Location estimated.
Road closure on Mount Baldy Road at Shinn Road. Northbound lanes are being shut down to assist with the evacuation of parties at Mount Baldy Village per the San Bernardino County Sheriffs Office.
Caltrans reports a hard closure of State Route 1 between Solomon Rd and Black Rd. Deep water is flowing across all lanes, rendering the roadway impassable.

Sepulveda Basin is flooded with at least one car stuck in flood waters.

Flooding reported in the right lane of Southbound La Cienega Boulevard. Later reports confirm minimum flooding in the number 3 lane. Closure signs were requested to mark the hazard.
Reported a mudflow on Frazier Mountain Road in the Ventura County mountains.
Roadway flooding reported on Eastbound SR-91 just west of Southbound I-605. Reporting party advises 12 to 14 inches of water in the slow lanes.

Roadway flooding reported at the Westbound I-10 off-ramp at Puente Avenue. A truck and car are stuck in deep water. FSP was unable to assist the truck due to water depth. Caltrans and CHP requested to assist with flooding and vehicle removal at 3:16 PM.
Major flooding reported at the 18th Street off-ramp from Eastbound I-10. A vehicle is stuck and potentially flooded out, blocking the ramp. The left lane of the off-ramp has been shut down.
Flooding reported at the Santa Anita Ave on-ramp to westbound I-10. Vehicle became stranded in deep standing water. On-ramp remains hazardous for low-clearance vehicles.
Tik Tok video showed significant roadway flooding on West Olive Avenue near the Ventura Freeway and Warner Bros. Studios.
Topanga Canyon Boulevard is closed from Pacific Coast Highway to Grand View Drive due to mud and debris flows.
Significant roadway flooding reported on the Westbound SR-60 transition to Northbound SR-71. Large areas of standing water are impacting the connector ramp.
Mud and rocks in the slow lane of the Sierra Highway.

Four vehicles became stuck in flooding at the intersection of South Santa Fe Avenue and South Reyes Avenue. Los Angeles County Fire Department (LACOFD) responded to assist occupants out of their vehicles. Public Works has been notified to unclog drains in the area.
A group of people had to be rescued when their car was swept away in flooded water in Palmdale. Location is estimated.
Mud slide reported on Northbound Bouquet Canyon Road at Texas Canyon Road. The slide is blocking Northbound lanes, specifically the number 2 lane of the two-lane roadway.
Roadway flooding reported at 150th Street East near Avenue M.

Road closure in the area of East Palmdale Boulevard and North Longview Road due to flooding. Multiple vehicles are reported stuck in the flood water. A command post has been established at Fire Station 114 at 39939 170th Street East.

Part of the roadway gave out on Sand Canyon Road at Santa Clara Truck Trail, resulting in an approximate 200-foot drop. A portion of the mountain also gave way and is on an additional part of the roadway. The Northbound lanes of Sand Canyon Road are affected.
Numerous rock and mud slides with flooding near Fairview Road in Ojai. Location is estimated.
Numerous rock and mud slides with flooding along Lockwood Valley Road. Location is estimated.

VERY WIDE AREA OF SWIFT RUNNING WATER ACROSS ROADWAY.
LA COUNTY FIRE RESCUED OVER 100 PEOPLE FROM FLOODING AND DEBRIS FLOWS, MANY FROM VEHICLES. MOST OCCURRED IN THE ANTELOPE VALLEY FOOTHILL AREAS NEAR VALYERMO, JUNIPER HILLS, PEARBLOSSOM, AND LLANO.
Roadway flooding reported on the transition road from Southbound I-110 to Westbound SR-91.
A mud slide was reported on a blind curve in the 3100 block of Lobo Canyon Road, approximately 1.3 miles west of Triunfo Canyon Road. The slide is blocking two-thirds of the roadway.

A driver in a blue car became stuck in flooding at 13170 East Avenue Q. The driver reported that something is blocking the roadway and they are unable to drive. The fire department has been requested to assist.
A vehicle with a person on top of it was reported floating away in the floodwaters at 43800 110th Street East. The Los Angeles County Fire Department has been notified and is responding to the scene.
A green truck became trapped in deep floodwaters near 107th St. E and E Avenue K. The Antelope Valley Sheriff’s Office (AVSO) requested CHP assistance to conduct a rescue for the stranded driver.
Significant flooding reported on Avenue I between 50th and 60th Street East. The roadway is submerged and impassable.

Flooding was reported on the Westbound I-10 just east of Fremont Avenue in Alhambra. Standing water is affecting the Fast Track (left) lane. A motorist reported their vehicle sustained damage after hitting unknown debris submerged in the floodwater.
Two vehicles—a silver sedan and a white SUV—became trapped in floodwaters. Each vehicle has one occupant inside. LACO Fire has been notified to assist with the rescue.
Reports of multiple vehicles trapped in floodwaters between Palmdale Blvd and Avenue T. One vehicle is reported to be overturned; it is currently unknown if occupants are inside.
Road Obstruction: A mudslide consisting of rocks and mud is blocking the northbound lane of Bouquet Canyon Road, approximately 14 miles south of Elizabeth Lake Road.
REPORTED AT SANTA BARBARA AIRPORT. AIRPORT FULLY CLOSED DOWN FOR SEVERAL HOURS.

The National Weather Service conducted a storm survey in the neighborhood of Boyle Heights in Los Angeles County, CA for damage associated with a low-topped mini supercell. Damage indicated a brief EF0 tornado occurred, with a maximum wind speed of 80 mph, a distance traveled of 0.3 miles, and a width of 30 yards.

A swift water rescue was successfully performed rescuing a man trapped under the US 101 bridge due to waters rapidly rising in the Ventura River. The southbound lanes of US 101 were closed for about an hour.
Flash flooding in the area lead to roadway closure and a vehicle stuck in water.

REPORTED AT OJAI VALLEY INN. OVERNIGHT SECURITY GAURD OBSERVED THAT THE SAN ANTIONI CREEK WAS FLOODED OVER GOLF FACILITIES INCLUDING ROADWAYS. THE SAN ANTONIO CREEK AT CAMP COMFORT REACHED MAJOR FLOOD STAGE WITH WATER OF 584.69 FEET AROUND THIS TIME.

A drainage canal failed and caused a sinkhole and erosion of the sides of the canal. Several side or backyards were affected with one home being yellow tagged by officials with their structure threatened.
Multiple drivers lost control on US 101 due to flooding and crashed. At least three vehicles were inoperable due to being submerged in several feet of water on a usually heavily trafficked offramp.
